text:
Inserts are strange, sort of. 
BTW the James Wood autograph is a Turquoise there are no Aquas in the set. It's worth about $175 - Golds are like $250-300 they're moving around some.
In the year of their production they float around $1 or maybe $2 if there is A LOT of buzz around a certain player.
Once the product rotates out, you'll still find packs floating around but the inserts price will drift more, closer to $2-3.
After like 3-5 years the product is a bit tougher to find and that's when you see any sort of real appreciation if it is there.
Higher end products inserts, can, seemingly on a whim spike without any prior warning.
Some Ohtani inserts I had when I initially pulled them were $2-3 cards raw but now are around $20 and they are from 2024/2025.
Either way it appears a lot of money is chasing BGS 10 Blacks now and snubbing PSA entirely. 
The price difference between a black 10 and a PSA 10 is astronomically different.

text:
Why Jasson Domínguez Hype Took Over Baseball Cards

People who are in the hobby probably get this, but like people who aren't wouldn't understand. It just it's not intuitive. Like Dominguez is 23 now. So he is officially like too old to be exciting now. Like that's the way prospecting works, right? Um if you look at it any year of Bowman, Bowman Chrome, Bowman draft products coming out, age is super critical to value. Like we we want the person to be young with as high a ceiling as possible. And that's what we chase. And I think that's the way everybody works. You know, if you get these college bats, say, coming out and they're 21, 22, 20, maybe 23 years old already, like that's one knock against them right there because they're not young enough to be the next guy.
title: Why Jasson Domínguez Hype Took Over Baseball Cards
